explanation: This transit anomaly occurred on a Wizz Air Malta flight (W46673) from Tirana, Albania to Comiso, Italy on January 11, 2024, with a single female passenger of Albanian nationality, aged between 46-60, who was traveling with a visa. The anomaly is flagged as "CRITICAL" due to an unusually high risk level, with a z-score of 10.80 and a ratio to baseline of 40.89, indicating a significant deviation from normal transit patterns, although the specific reason for the anomaly is marked as "unknown" and the outcome of the control is also "unknown".

This transit anomaly occurred on a Wizz Air Malta flight (W43863) from Tirana, Albania to Pescara, Italy on January 7, 2024, with 21 individuals triggering alarms, primarily due to manual alerts. The anomaly is considered high-risk due to a significantly elevated z-score (7.48) and ratio to baseline (28.62), indicating a notable deviation from typical transit patterns, warranting further investigation.

The transit anomaly occurred on a Wizz Air Malta flight (W43875) from Tirana, Albania to Rimini, Italy on January 9, 2024, with 27 travelers triggering alarms, resulting in a high risk level due to an unusually high number of alerts. The anomaly is characterized by a z-score of 7.11 and a ratio to baseline of 27.26, indicating a significant deviation from the norm, with all 119 passengers investigated and 20 triggering alarms, although the specific reason for the alarms and the outcome of the investigation are currently unknown.

This transit anomaly occurred on a Wizz Air Malta flight (W43855) from Tirana, Albania to Ancona, Italy on January 2, 2024, and was flagged as high-risk due to an Interpol alert. The flight was investigated and the passenger, a female Albanian national between 46-60 years old, was denied entry (respinto) after landing in Ancona, with the transit being classified as a single-leg journey (singola tratta) with an unusually high risk level.

This transit anomaly involves a single flight (W46637) from Tirana, Albania to Genova, Italy on February 19, 2024, with a passenger flagged for investigation due to a known profile ('passeggero noto') and an alert triggered by the SDI (a law enforcement or intelligence system). The anomaly is classified as 'HIGH' risk due to a statistically significant deviation from the baseline, with a z-score of 6.74 and a ratio to baseline of 25.90, indicating that this event is unusually suspicious compared to typical patterns.
